questions.stem: Sorting a column of text in ascending order arranges it how?
questions.explanation: For text, ascending order runs from A to Z. For numbers, ascending order runs from smallest to largest.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Sorting numbers in descending order arranges them how?
questions.explanation: Descending order for numbers runs from the largest value down to the smallest.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The names Mensah, Asare and Boateng are sorted in ascending order. What is the resulting order?
questions.explanation: Ascending text order is alphabetical from A to Z, so Asare comes before Boateng, which comes before Mensah.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In a multi-level sort, which level is treated as the primary sort key?
options: The bottom level in the list
The top level in the list
The level with the most data
questions.explanation: The Sort dialog applies levels from the top down, so the topmost level is the primary key and lower levels only break ties within equal primary values.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You want records grouped by Department from A to Z, and within each department by Salary from highest to lowest. How should the sort levels be set?
questions.explanation: Department must be the first, primary level so records group by department, and Salary descending must be the second level to order the rows inside each department.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What does turning on Filter (AutoFilter) add to the header row of a data range?
options: A drop-down arrow on each column heading
questions.explanation: The Filter command places a drop-down arrow button on each column header, and these arrows open the menus used to choose which rows to display.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When you apply a filter, what happens to the rows that do not match the criteria?
options: They are hidden but remain in the sheet
They move to a new sheet
questions.explanation: Filtering hides the non-matching rows rather than deleting them. Clearing the filter brings every row back into view unchanged.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How can you tell at a glance that a filter is currently active on a particular column?
options: A small funnel icon appears on that column's drop-down arrow
questions.explanation: A funnel symbol on the column's filter button signals that the column is filtering the data, helping you spot which fields are limiting the view.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A Number Filter of Greater Than 50 is applied to the values 30, 55, 80 and 45. Which values remain visible?
questions.explanation: Greater Than 50 keeps only values strictly above 50, which are 55 and 80. The values 30 and 45 are hidden.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which of these is a Text Filter option rather than a Number Filter option?
questions.explanation: Begins With is a Text Filter that matches the start of entries. Greater Than, Between and Above Average are number-oriented filters.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In a Custom AutoFilter, what do the And and Or options let you do?
options: Combine two criteria on the same column
questions.explanation: The Custom AutoFilter dialog lets you set two conditions on one column and join them with And, where both must hold, or Or, where either may hold.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A custom number filter keeps rows where the value is Greater Than 100 OR Less Than 10. From the values 5, 50 and 150, which remain visible?
questions.explanation: With an OR join, a row stays if either condition is met. 5 is less than 10 and 150 is greater than 100, so both stay, while 50 meets neither and is hidden.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In Excel filter criteria, what does the wildcard character ? represent?
questions.explanation: The question mark wildcard stands for exactly one character in any position, so b?ll matches ball, bell and bill.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In Excel filter criteria, what does the wildcard character * (asterisk) represent?
options: Any number of characters, including none
questions.explanation: The asterisk matches any sequence of characters of any length, including an empty one, so Beg* matches Begin, Beginning and Beg.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What does the Top 10 number filter let you do, despite its name?
options: Show only exactly ten rows always
Show the top or bottom items by a number you choose, as items or percent
questions.explanation: The Top 10 filter is flexible. You can pick Top or Bottom, type any count, and choose Items or Percent, so it is not limited to ten.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is the key difference between sorting and filtering a list?
options: Sorting reorders rows while filtering hides rows that do not match
Filtering reorders rows while sorting hides them
questions.explanation: Sorting rearranges the order of the rows, keeping all of them visible, whereas filtering leaves the order alone but hides the rows that fail the criteria.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Why does Excel keep entire rows together when you sort a properly structured data range?
options: To keep each record's fields aligned in the same row
questions.explanation: Each row is a single record, so Excel moves the whole row as a unit during a sort to keep every field, such as name, age and salary, matched to the correct person.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What does the My data has headers checkbox in the Sort dialog do when ticked?
options: Keeps the first row out of the sort and uses it as column labels
questions.explanation: Ticking My data has headers tells Excel the top row contains labels, so it stays in place and its names appear in the Sort by list instead of being sorted with the data.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You select only one column of a multi-column table and choose Sort. What does the Sort Warning offer?
options: To expand the selection so related columns sort along with it
questions.explanation: The Sort Warning offers to Expand the selection, so the adjacent columns move together and records stay intact, rather than sorting the one column in isolation and scrambling the rows.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Besides sorting by values, the Sort On list in the Sort dialog also lets you sort by what?
questions.explanation: The Sort On option supports Cell Color, Font Color and Conditional Formatting Icon in addition to Cell Values, which is useful when colour marks categories.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How do you remove a filter from one column while keeping filters on other columns?
options: Open that column's drop-down and choose Clear Filter
questions.explanation: Opening the column's filter menu and choosing Clear Filter from that field removes only its filter, leaving any filters on other columns in place.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A Text Filter set to Contains the word North is applied. Which entry would remain visible?
questions.explanation: Contains North keeps any entry that includes the text North anywhere in it, so North East Zone stays while entries without that text are hidden.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: After applying several filters, which command quickly redisplays every row in the table?
options: Clear in the Sort and Filter group
questions.explanation: The Clear command in the Sort and Filter group on the Data tab removes all active filters at once and shows every row again, without turning the filter arrows off.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When you filter on a Date column, which choice is a built-in Date Filter?
questions.explanation: Date Filters include relative periods such as Today, This Week and Last Month, which select rows by date without typing exact dates.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible
